species.notes: Note by John Edward Matulina Villasis (30-Aug-24): The specimen by Ridsdale, Baquiran et al. ISU440 (L) represent Palaquium pseudorostratum, a species that is often confused with Palaquium pseudocuneatum both in the field and from sterile specimens, but it is not impossible that Palaquium pseudocuneatum and many more or less clearly intermediate forms between the two species are present in parts of the country closer to Borneo.
